---
description: "GitHub Agentic Workflows authoring guidance — Markdown-based AI automation for GitHub Actions"
applyTo: "**/.github/workflows/*.md"
---

# GitHub Agentic Workflows

GitHub Agentic Workflows are Markdown-based repository automations that run AI coding agents inside GitHub Actions with strong guardrails.

## Workflow File Structure

Every agentic workflow is a `.md` file in `.github/workflows/` with two parts:

1. **YAML Frontmatter** (`---` delimiters) — trigger, permissions, tools, safe-outputs
2. **Markdown Body** — natural language instructions describing the automation goal

A compiled `.lock.yml` file is generated by `gh aw compile` and must be committed alongside the `.md` file.

## Frontmatter Reference

```yaml
---
name: Workflow Name              # Human-readable name
description: What this does      # Brief purpose statement
on:
  schedule: daily                # daily | weekly | or cron expression
  workflow_dispatch:             # Allow manual trigger
  slash_command:                 # /command-name trigger from comments
    name: command-name
  reaction: "eyes"              # Reaction emoji on trigger

permissions:
  contents: read                 # Read-only by default — principle of least privilege
  issues: read
  pull-requests: read

safe-outputs:                    # Pre-approved write operations
  create-issue:
    title-prefix: "[prefix] "
    labels: [label1, label2]
    expires: 2d
  create-pull-request:
    title-prefix: "[prefix] "
    labels: [label1]
    draft: true
    reviewers: [copilot]
  add-comment:
    target: "*"

tools:
  github:
    toolsets: [default]          # GitHub MCP tools
  edit:                          # File editing capability
  bash: true                     # Shell command execution
  web-fetch:                     # HTTP requests
  repo-memory: true              # Persistent memory across runs

network:
  allowed:
    - defaults

timeout-minutes: 20
---
```

## Authoring Rules

- **Read-only by default**: Only grant write access through `safe-outputs`, never through `permissions`.
- **One goal per workflow**: Keep each workflow focused on a single automation objective.
- **Be specific about outputs**: Define `title-prefix`, `labels`, and `expires` for every safe-output.
- **Include evidence requirements**: Instruct the agent to back findings with real data (logs, CLI output, diffs).
- **Add exit conditions**: Define when the workflow should do nothing and exit gracefully.
- **Use structured output**: Define the report/comment format explicitly in the Markdown body.
- **Compile after changes**: Always run `gh aw compile` after modifying a workflow `.md` file.
- **Commit both files**: Both the `.md` and the `.lock.yml` must be committed together.

## Safe Output Types

| Output | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `create-issue` | Open a new issue with findings |
| `create-pull-request` | Open a PR with code changes |
| `add-comment` | Comment on existing issues/PRs |
| `update-issue` | Modify an existing issue |
| `push-to-pull-request-branch` | Push commits to an existing PR branch |

## Patterns

- **DailyOps**: Scheduled workflows for maintenance (doc updates, test improvements, reports)
- **IssueOps**: Triggered by issue events for triage, labeling, routing
- **ChatOps**: Slash commands in comments for on-demand tasks (`/test-assist`, `/review`)
- **ProjectOps**: Project board automation and cross-repo coordination
